display_name: ADC — Immune stimulator payload (STING / TLR agonist)
source: Emerging clinical data; preclinical literature on XMT-2056, SBT6050 class
description: Targeted delivery of innate immune agonists (STING, TLR7/8) to tumor microenvironment. Expected to produce systemic immune activation when payload is released. Toxicity profile is immune-mediated, not cytotoxic — fundamentally different from all other ADC payload classes.

expected_findings.severity_threshold: Severe cytokine storm with hemodynamic instability = adverse. Grade 3+ CRS = never reclassify.
expected_findings.rationale: Systemic innate immune activation from STING/TLR agonist release. Acute phase proteins (CRP, fibrinogen, haptoglobin) are downstream markers.
Lymphocyte redistribution during immune activation. Expected to be transient and self-resolving.
Hepatic immune activation from systemically distributed STING/TLR agonist.
Lymphoid organ activation from innate immune stimulation.

expected_findings.description: Injection site reaction (immune-mediated, may be more severe than non-immune ADCs)
expected_findings.rationale: Local innate immune activation at injection site. May be more severe than standard ADC injection site reactions.

expected_findings.severity_threshold: Refractory fever requiring ICU intervention = adverse. Grade 1-2 is expected.
expected_findings.rationale: Cytokine-driven fever from innate immune activation. Expected class effect of STING/TLR agonist payloads.
